sanskrit:
आब्रह्मभुवनाल्लोकाः पुनरावर्तिनोऽर्जुन।
मामुपेत्य तु कौन्तेय पुनर्जन्म न विद्यते॥
transliteration:
ā-brahma-bhuvanāl lokāḥ punar-āvartino ’rjuna |
mām upetya tu kaunteya punar janma na vidyate ||
literal_meaning: Up to Brahmaloka, all worlds are subject to return, Arjuna; but having reached Me, O son-of-Kunti, rebirth is not found.
contextual_meaning: Every achievement, status, or pleasure within the material spectrum is cyclical and impermanent; only union with the transcendent Self (Kṛṣṇa) ends the compulsive loop of becoming.
psychological_state: Post-achievement let-down; the mind sees that every peak experience is followed by descent, provoking a search for a non-relapsing state.
trigger_scenario: You finally land the dream job/relationship/physique, yet the old anxiety quietly returns—realising that no external milestone can stop the internal treadmill.
anchor_text: Beyond every cycle, rest in the changeless Self.
